fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Virginia CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Virginia - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity virginia, how to transfer CNA license to virginia, virginia CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Virginia.

steps.title: Complete a State-Approved Training Program
steps.description: Finish an Virginia-approved CNA training program, usually 120 hours long.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Exam
steps.description: Pass both the written and clinical skills portions of the Virginia nurse aide exam.

steps.title: Submit Your Application
steps.description: Apply for certification, complete any required background check steps, and pay the required fees.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: Who administers the Virginia CNA exam?
faqs.answer: The VA nurse aide exam is administered by Credentia. Register at credentia.com/test-takers/va.

faqs.question: How do I verify a Virginia CNA?
faqs.answer: Use the DHP License Lookup at dhp.virginiainteractive.org/Lookup/Index or contact VBON at (804) 367-4515 or nurseaide@dhp.virginia.gov.

reciprocity.notes: Virginia CNA endorsement is handled through the Credentia CNA365 platform or the VA Board of Nursing.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in VA.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Virginia.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Virginia
hero.dek: Virginia CNA certification requires a 120-hour training program, Virginia Board of Nursing eligibility, and a passing score on the NNAAP (Pearson VUE) exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Virginia
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Virginia CNA certification.
